guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: fluid/electrolyte and urinary retention
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: intractable vomiting, distension and orthostatic collapse mimic mechanical bowel obstruction, hypovolaemic shock and DKA.
Important-not-to-miss: significant dehydration/shock, a surgical abdomen, and urinary retention (palpable bladder) all need urgent in-person management.
Anchoring: 'known hEDS visceral flare' should not close off a surgical abdomen or under-weight dehydration/retention.
Commission trap: IM antiemetic and small-sip oral rehydration do not translate to remote management of a shocked, retaining patient.
Telehealth limits: abdominal/bladder examination, BP and IV therapy/catheterisation cannot be done over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
